misra_context.description: A function parameter should not be modified
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.8 (Advisory): A function parameter should not be modified. This code violates Rule 17.8 because a function parameter should not be modified. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: A function shall not be declared implicitly
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.3 (Mandatory): A function shall not be declared implicitly. This code violates Rule 17.3 because a function shall not be declared implicitly. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: The function argument corresponding to a parameter declared to have an array type shall have an appropriate number of elements
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 17.5 (Advisory): The function argument corresponding to a parameter declared to have an array type shall have an appropriate number of elements. This code violates Rule 17.5 because the function argument corresponding to a parameter declared to have an array type shall have an appropriate number of elements. Function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.

misra_context.description: The right hand operand of a shift operator shall lie between zero and one less than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand
misra_context.guidelines: Rule 12.2 (Required): The right hand operand of a shift operator shall lie between zero and one less than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand. This code violates Rule 12.2 because the right hand operand of a shift operator shall lie between zero and one less than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand. Expression complexity and unsafe shifts can hide errors and make evaluation harder to reason about.

Rule 10.3 (Required): Implicit narrowing conversion. The value of 'u16val' (uint16_t) is assigned to 'u8val' (uint8_t). This implicit narrowing conversion may silently truncate the value (300 → 44), causing data loss and incorrect program behaviour.
